title: Compress Sorted Stream
description:
Given a nondecreasing array, remove duplicates in place so that each unique value appears once. Return the new length. The first part of the array should contain the deduplicated values in order.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^9
explanation:
Intuition
A write pointer tracks the next position to place a new unique value. Scan with a read pointer and copy values when they differ from the previous item.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Handle empty input as length 0.
2. Main Algorithm: Set write to 1 and scan read from 1 to end, copying on changes.
3. Return Result: Return write as the new length.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n) because the scan adjusts pointers a limited
  number of times and avoids nested loops.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables or an output
  list are used.

Key Insights
- Two pointers separate read and write roles
- Only copies when a new value appears

Common Pitfalls
- Overwriting before reading can lose values
- Forgetting empty case handling
hints: Keep a write pointer for the next unique slot
Copy only when nums[read] differs from nums[read-1]
Return the count of unique values
